content: Sexually transmitted infections (STIs) remain major public health concern. Gonorrhea (Neisseria gonorrhoeae): urethritis, cervicitis, pharyngitis, proctitis; complications include PID, epididymitis, disseminated gonococcal infection; increasing antimicrobial resistance (cephalosporin-resistant strains emerging). Syphilis (Treponema pallidum): primary (painless chancre), secondary (maculopapular rash including palms/soles, condyloma lata), latent (seropositive without symptoms), tertiary (gummas, cardiovascular, neurosyphilis); congenital syphilis rates rising. Chlamydia (C. trachomatis): most common bacterial STI, often asymptomatic; causes PID, infertility, reactive arthritis. HSV: lifelong infection with recurrent outbreaks. HPV: causes genital warts and cancers (cervical, anal, oropharyngeal); vaccines prevent high-risk types.
